context.p: The mptcpd service is a component of the mptcp protocol which provides an instrument to configure mptcp endpoints. The mptcpd service creates a subflow endpoint for each address by default. The endpoint list is updated dynamically according to IP addresses modification on the running host. The mptcpd service creates the list of endpoints automatically. It enables multiple paths as an alternative to using the ip utility.

step.cmd: Enable mptcp.enabled option in the kernel with the following command:
To stop the mptcpd service, use the following command:
To configure mptcpd service manually, modify the /etc/mptcpd/mptcpd.conf configuration file.

info.p: Note, that the endpoint, which mptcpd service creates, lasts till the host shutdown.
